cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why small pieces of space junk threaten satellites. The options are A through D. First, I need to refer back to the text. The article mentions that even bits of space junk as small as paint dots can be a threat. The reason given is that this debris orbits at speeds up to 28,200 kilometers per hour. The high speed is why tiny paint dots have damaged space shuttle windshields, necessitating replacements. So the key point here is the high speed. Let's check the options. Option A says they travel at a very high speed. That matches the explanation in the text. Option B is about tracking, but the text doesn't mention difficulty in tracking as the reason for the threat.
